data.title: Validate and improve root-level onboarding documentation for DSO plugin
data.description:
## Context
New users have no functional entry point for understanding or installing the Digital Service Orchestra plugin. The project root README is nearly empty — containing only placeholder text and a single optional dependency snippet — and there is no INSTALL file at the root. The only installation documentation lives at plugins/dso/docs/INSTALL.md, buried inside the plugin directory and written for the git-clone path superseded by marketplace-based installation. Engineers must discover this internal document through trial and error; non-engineers have no resource at all. A new platform engineer joining a team that uses DSO is the primary persona. This epic produces static, human-authored documentation (distinct from dso-q0lo scope which addresses LLM-generated artifact volume).

## Success Criteria
- A gap audit report documents what is missing, inaccurate, or incomplete across README, INSTALL, and CONFIGURATION-REFERENCE.md before any edits are made. The report includes a command verification appendix with two parts: (a) results of executing each standalone shell command in INSTALL.md verbatim in a clean shell session; (b) a manual confirmation that the /plugin marketplace add and /plugin install commands match the syntax in the official plugin marketplace documentation.
- Root README.md describes what DSO does in plain language understandable to a non-engineer, and contains a link to INSTALL.md.
- Root INSTALL.md provides step-by-step marketplace installation instructions (/plugin marketplace add navapbc/digital-service-orchestra + /plugin install dso@digital-service-orchestra), with required and optional dependencies listed separately, macOS homebrew installation steps, and a link to the official homebrew install guide at brew.sh.
- INSTALL.md includes a description of /dso:onboarding with realistic time expectations and a link to the configuration reference.
- INSTALL.md contains an Integration Setup section with: (a) a Jira subsection listing the required API token, user email, and Jira base URL with a link to the Atlassian API token guide; (b) a Figma subsection listing the required personal access token with a link to the Figma personal access token guide; (c) a Confluence subsection containing a single placeholder sentence stating the integration is planned with no actionable steps.
- plugins/dso/docs/INSTALL.md is removed and all internal references updated to point to root INSTALL.md — CLAUDE.md must be updated before deletion to avoid triggering the referential integrity pre-commit gate; .test-index entries mapping to the removed file are cleaned up.
- An automated scan of all files under plugins/dso/ AND .claude/dso-config.conf and the example config file for configuration key reads (including keys read via inline config functions, not only read-config.sh invocations) produces a gap list in the gap audit report; every key on that list absent from CONFIGURATION-REFERENCE.md is added with a description before epic closure.
- All hyperlinks in edited and added documentation resolve without error (link check during accuracy review pass); a team member not involved in authoring performs a walkthrough of root README and INSTALL.md and confirms the steps are followable to the /dso:onboarding entry point without needing additional context.

## Scenario Analysis
- Removing plugins/dso/docs/INSTALL.md breaks .test-index boundary test coverage (high, deployment): .test-index maps INSTALL.md to test-reinstall-hooks scripts; stale entry corrupts test gate tracking. Addressed in SC6.
- CLAUDE.md hard-links to plugins/dso/docs/INSTALL.md — referential integrity gate failure (critical, deployment): deleting without updating CLAUDE.md first blocks all subsequent commits. Addressed in SC6.
- Config key scan misses namespaces only in dso-config.conf defaults (high, configuration): brainstorm.enforce_entry_gate, test_gate.*, worktree.isolation_enabled etc. will not surface in a skills-only grep. Addressed in SC7.
- Keys read via inline _read_config_key() functions missed by standard grep (medium, configuration): ticket-create.sh, ticket-clarity-check.sh use local readers not caught by read-config.sh grep. Addressed in SC7.
- INSTALL.md step 3 copy commands use $CLAUDE_PLUGIN_ROOT which is unset in most installs (high, runtime): cp commands silently fail; new INSTALL.md must not reproduce this pattern.
- VERSIONING.md shows working plugin install commands that conflict with INSTALL.md aspirational label (high, runtime): new root INSTALL.md must reconcile against official plugin marketplace documentation.
- Non-engineer users need bash >= 4.0 and GNU coreutils before onboarding — macOS out-of-the-box silently fails (high, runtime): required deps in SC3 must list bash and coreutils as blocking prerequisites appearing before the /dso:onboarding step.

## Research Findings
- Jira API token guide: https://support.atlassian.com/atlassian-account/docs/manage-api-tokens-for-your-atlassian-account/
- Figma personal access token guide: https://help.figma.com/hc/en-us/articles/8085703771159-Manage-personal-access-tokens
- Homebrew install guide: https://brew.sh/
- Marketplace install documentation: https://code.claude.com/docs/en/plugin-marketplaces

## Dependencies
- Hard coordination: 9b59-bac7 (plugin boundary allowlist — needs update when plugins/dso/docs/INSTALL.md is removed)
- Soft coordination: d97a-6bb7 (/dso:doctor — confirm it does not reference old INSTALL.md path before removal)

## Approach
Audit-first — gap-analyze all three artifacts (README, INSTALL, CONFIGURATION-REFERENCE.md) before writing. The gap report (including command verification appendix) is the walking skeleton deliverable. Writing and consolidation happen against confirmed gaps only, eliminating wasted rewrites.

### Planning Intelligence Log
- Web research (Step 2.6): triggered
  - Bright-line conditions that fired: security/authentication/credentials (INSTALL.md will link to Jira, Figma, Confluence API key guides); user-requested marketplace research
- Scenario analysis (Step 2.75): triggered
  - Scenarios surviving blue team filter: 7
- Practitioner-requested additional cycles: none
- Follow-on scrutiny (Step 0): not triggered
- Feasibility resolution (Step 2.5): not triggered
- LLM-instruction signal (Step 5): not triggered (doc-epic exclusion applied)
